summary: Git init, .gitignore, .gitattributes, first commit, annotated master tag and phase/1-foundation branch are all correctly in place and match patterns.md (master=tag, no main yet, work on phase branch). A few minor gaps remain: Android Studio IDE variants (.idea/*.xml overrides, *.iml captured but no navigation/Captures folders edge cases), missing Gradle cache variants, and .gitattributes lacks gradlew.bat-style linting safety net plus a few Android binary formats.

findings.issue: .gitignore misses a few common Android/Gradle artefacts that will show up once Task 2 runs Gradle: `.kotlin/` (Kotlin 2.x incremental cache), `.gradle-cache/`, `hs_err_pid*.log` (JVM crash dumps), and `proguard/` output dirs. Also no entry for `app/release/`, `app/debug/`, or `*.apk`/`*.aab` — once Task 2+ starts producing binaries they may accidentally get staged.
findings.suggestion: Add to the Android section: `*.apk`, `*.aab`, `*.ap_`, `*.dex`, `app/release/`, `app/debug/`. Add to Gradle section: `.kotlin/`, `hs_err_pid*.log`. Cheap to add now, avoids churn in Task 2.

findings.issue: `.idea/` is fully ignored, which is Android Studio's recommended default, but the AOSP/Google convention (and Google AI Edge Gallery upstream) keeps `.idea/codeStyles/` and `.idea/inspectionProfiles/` in VCS so the team shares formatter/inspection rules. Since PhoneWrap is a fork of that project, cloning upstream's `.idea/codeStyles/` later will be silently blocked by the current rule.
findings.suggestion: Consider the Android-Studio pattern: ignore `.idea/` broadly but un-ignore `!.idea/codeStyles/` and `!.idea/inspectionProfiles/` (and `!.idea/icon.png` if the fork ships one). Not blocking — can be deferred until actually needed.

findings.issue: Binary list is solid but missing a few Android-ecosystem formats that will appear later: `*.aab`, `*.apk`, `*.dex`, `*.bin`, `*.onnx`, `*.gguf`, `*.woff2`. Also `gradlew.bat` is not pinned — it's a .bat so the `*.bat text eol=crlf` rule covers it, but making the intent explicit (`gradlew.bat text eol=crlf`) mirrors the explicit `gradlew text eol=lf` line and is easier to audit.
findings.suggestion: Append `*.apk binary`, `*.aab binary`, `*.dex binary`, `*.gguf binary`, `*.onnx binary`, and add an explicit `gradlew.bat text eol=crlf` line next to the existing gradlew rule.

findings.issue: Annotated `master` tag message is `initial zero state` — fine, but patterns.md § Tags suggests slightly more descriptive tag messages that note what the tag pins (e.g. `master: empty repository baseline before phase-1`). Not a blocker, purely stylistic.
findings.suggestion: Optional: re-tag with `git tag -a -f master -m "master: baseline before phase-1 foundation work"`. Skip if team prefers terse tag messages.

findings.issue: Creating `phase/1-foundation` via `git symbolic-ref HEAD` before the first commit (so master exists only as a tag, never as a branch) is an elegant execution of patterns.md intent, but it deviates from the literal hint order in task 1.md. Worth a one-line note in the task's completion record so future readers understand why `git branch -a` shows no `master` branch.
findings.suggestion: Add a one-liner to the task completion log explaining the symbolic-ref-first approach and why it better matches patterns.md (master = tag-only).
